semanticCore: Refuting, invalidating, slipping, falsifying, vanishing, or slippery path
quranicRelevance: Refuting truth with falsehood (Yud-hidoo - 'liyud-hidoo bihi al-haqqa'), and arguments being invalid ('dahihah').

keyDerivations.meaning: To refute, invalidate, nullify, make slip, or defeat argument
Invalid, void, refuted, slipping, or baseless

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَالَّذِينَ يُحَاجُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا اسْتُجِيبَ لَهُ حُجَّتُهُمْ دَاحِضَةٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ (42:16): And those who argue concerning God after He has been acknowledged - their argument is invalid/void before their Lord.
summaryEn: The root represents invalidating arguments, condemning the futile attempts of disbelievers to refute (Yud-hidoo) truth using baseless sophistry.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الزلق والإبطال للمكان والمكانة، واصفاً محاولات الكفار لإبطال ومحو معالم الحق بالجدال الباطل (ليُدحضوا)، وحججهم الزالقة الباطلة.
rawText: ادحض 4 , (A, TA,) inf. n. إِدْحَاضٌ, (S,) He made (S, A, TA) a man's foot, (A, TA,) and a man, (TA,) to slip; (S, A, TA;) as also 🡻دَحَضَ. (TA.) === It (rain) made a place slippery. (TA.) === ‡ He (God, S, Msb, or a man, K) annulled, or rendered null or void, an argument, a plea, an allegation, or an evidence: (S, Msb, K:) he rebutted it; as also 🡻دَحَضَ, inf. n. دَحْضٌ. (TA; but in this instance, only the inf. n. of the latter verb is mentioned.) Thus in the Kur [xviii. 54, and xl. 5], لِيُدْحِضُوا بِهِ الحَقَّ ‡ That they may, or might, rebut thereby the truth. (TA.) دَاحِضٌ [Slipping] has for a pl. دُحْضٌ: occur- ring in a trad., in the phrase دُحْضُ الأَقْدَامِ, meaning † those having no firmness, or stability, nor determination, or resolution, in affairs. (TA.) === حُجَّتُهُمْ دَاحِضَةٌ, in the Kur [xlii. 15], means ‡ Their argument, or plea, or allegation, is null, or void: or, as IDrd says, on the authority of AO; it means مَدْحُوضَةٌ [rebutted]. (TA.) دَحَضٌ : see the next preceding paragraph. دَحَضَتْ رِجْلُهُ 1 , (S, A, K,) aor. ﹷ, (S,) inf. n. دَحْضٌ (S, A) and دُحُوضٌ, (A, TA,) His foot slipped. (S, A, K.) And دَحَضَ said of a man, He slipped. (Msb.) === دَحَضَتِ الشَّمْسُ, (S, A, K,) aor. and inf. ns. as above, (TA,) ‡ The sun declined (S, A, K) towards the place of setting, (TA,) عَنْ كَبِدِ السَّمَآءُ (S) or عن بَطْنِ السمآء (A) [from the meridian]; as though it slipped. (TA.) === دَحَضَتْ حُجَّتُهُ, (S, A, Msb, K,) aor. ﹷ, (Msb,) inf. n. دُحُوضٌ, (S, K,) or دَحْضٌ, (Msb,) ‡ His argument, plea, allegation, or evidence, was, or became, null, or void. (S, A, * Msb, K.) ___ See also 4, in two places. دَحُوضٌ : see the next preceding paragraph. دَحْضٌ A slippery place; as also 🡻دَحَضٌ (S, K) and 🡻دَحُوضٌ (O, K) and 🡻مِدْحَضٌ (Freytag, from the Deewán of the Hudhalees,) [or مَدْحَضٌ?]: pl. دِحَاضٌ, (K,) [a pl. of the second,] like as جِبَالٌ is pl. of جَبَلٌ. (TA.) === Also Water that causes slipping: pl. أَدْحَاضٌ. (TA.) مَدْحَضَةٌ A cause of slipping; syn. مَزَلَّةٌ. (K.) You say, هٰذِهِ مَدْحَضَةٌ لِلْقَدَمِ [This is a cause of slipping to the foot]. (A.) And مَكَانٌ مَدْحَضَةٌ, in a trad. respecting the Sirát, means A place on which the feet will not remain firm. (TA.) مِدْحَاضٌ A place in which one slips much, or often: pl. مَدَاحِضُ. (TA.) مِدْحَضٌ [or مَدْحَضٌ?]: see دَحْضٌ.
